creator: gobledb@gmail.com
text:
When I launch pf daemon, I have a kernel panic.
Boot with it (/etc/rc.conf) or manually (# /etc/rc.d/pf start)

Note: The world as kernel was compiled and instaled today.

How-To-Repeat: # /etc/rc.d/pf start
Starting pf...
panic: mutex pf task mtx owned at /usr/src/sys/contrib/pf/net/if_pfsync.c:3163
cpuid = 0
KDB: enter: panic
[ thread pid 1853 tid 100066 ]
Stopped at Kdb_enter+0x3b: movq $0,0x60e442 (%rip)

------------------------------------Using db---------------------------------
db > examine
Kdb_enter+0x3b: 4205c748
-----------------------------------------------
db > print
ffffffff80522ddb
-----------------------------------------------
db > match
After 4 instructions ( 0 loads, 0 stones )
Stopped at Kdb_enter+0x4c: net
-----------------------------------------------
db > countfreebuf
Counted 51320 free, 47 used (51367 tot)
numfreebuffer is 51320
-----------------------------------------------
db > next
After 889 instructions (0 loads 0 stones)
[ thread pid 0 tid 100048 ]
Stopped at spinlock_exit+0x39: net
-----------------------------------------------
db > trace
Tracing pid 0 tid 100048 td 0xfffffe 0002604460
spinlock_exit() at spinlock_exit+0x39
taskqueue_thread_loop() at taskqueue_thread_loop+0x5f
fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x135
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0xe
---trap0, rip = 0, rsp = 0xffffff8221da0d00, vbp = 0---
------------------------------------------------
db > until
[ thread pid 0 tid 100048 ]
Stopped at msleep_spin+0x183: call_mtx_assent
------------------------------------------------
db > step
[ thread pid 0 tid 100048 ]
Stopped at msleep_spin+0x209_ jmp msleep_spin+0x151
